nameMeanings.name: Hotham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (East Yorkshire): habitational name usually from Hotham (East Yorkshire), but occasionally perhaps also from Holtham in Legsby (Lincolnshire). The East Yorkshire placename may derive from Old English hōdum, dative plural form of hōd ‘shelter’, while the origin of the Lincolnshire placename is uncertain. Compare Holtham .

variants.name: Holtham
meanings: habitational name from a place so named or a topographic name for someone who lived ‘(at the) enclosure in the wood’, from Middle English holt ‘wood, grove’ + ham ‘enclosure’ (Old English holt + hamm). Compare Haltom and Holtom .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

en.lifeSummary: When Amphelicia Alice Hotham was born in 1320, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Geoffrey Hotham, was 20 and her mother, Mrs. Geoffrey Hotham, was 26. She had at least 1 son and 2 daughters with Sir Alexander Cave. She died in 1361, in Hotham, Yorkshire East Riding, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 41.
